question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newTracingResponseWriter()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the tracingResponseWriter is allocated but the inner handler panics, causing the deferred log write to still execute with a partially initialized struct, leading to a nil pointer dereference and process crash?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths containing format verbs (e.g., %s, %d, %v), and since fmt.Sprintf uses the path as a value argument (not a format string), verify whether any indirect use of the path in logging could trigger format string misinterpretation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that are exactly equal to internalPaths keys but with different casing (e.g., /Health/Liveness), causing them to be logged at Info level instead of Debug, amplifying log volume and exhausting disk?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an X-Forwarded-For header containing thousands of IP addresses separated by commas, causing the entire multi-kilobyte string to be stored in ipAddress and embedded in every log entry, exhausting disk space?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that contain binary data (non-UTF8 bytes), causing log.Info(message) to write binary data to the log file, corrupting it and crashing log consumers that expect valid UTF-8?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Write()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the inner handler to call Write() with a very large byte slice, and since tracingResponseWriter stores the full slice in w.data without any size limit, cause memory exhaustion when many concurrent requests each store large response bodies?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that are valid but contain repeated query parameters (e.g., ?a=1&a=1&...&a=1 thousands of times), causing request.URL.RequestURI() to return a very long string that is logged without truncation, exhausting disk?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any connection or request timeout enforcement in TracingMiddleware to hold connections open indefinitely, causing tracingResponseWriter objects to remain allocated in memory and exhaust heap?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with an X-Real-IP header containing only whitespace characters, which passes the len(ipAddress) == 0 check (since len > 0), causing whitespace to be logged as the client IP and masking the true source of a DoS attack?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that are valid Rosetta API endpoints but with extremely long path parameters (e.g., /block/transaction/<64KB string>), causing request.URL.RequestURI() to return a very long string that is logged without truncation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that contain logrus field separator characters, causing log.Info(message) to produce malformed structured log entries that corrupt log aggregation systems and cause them to crash?
